summary: Retest of the two CRITICAL iteration-4 regressions (judge mis-scoring grounded answers + per-user cache pollution across threads). Both fixes verified end-to-end. 15/15 backend tests pass: 11/11 in original test_uploads.py and 4/4 in new test_uploads_regression.py. (a) Upload-grounded RAG now works: token from uploaded file appears in done.final_answer, judge_scores.best_index points to local_retrieval, uploads_used emitted before agent_start. (b) Cache is correctly skipped on upload-bearing threads — repeat identical question on an upload thread runs the engine both times (cache_check.hit=false, done.cache_hit=false) and stays grounded. (c) Cross-thread isolation works: a no-upload thread can cache, but a separate thread with uploads asking the same question skips cache and runs agents. (d) Standard no-uploads flow still caches normally (miss → hit on repeat).

minor.issue: Returns 200 with {'ok': true} instead of REST-idiomatic 204 No Content (carried over from iter-4; non-blocking).
Returns empty list when caller doesn't own the thread instead of 404 (carried over from iter-4; safe but silent).

critical_code_review_comments: stream.py:198-205 — cache lookup correctly gated by upload_count==0. Good fix. Counting uploads once via db.uploaded_files.count_documents is cheap and authoritative.
stream.py:438-439 — cache.add() is now skipped when has_uploads. Prevents future poisoning across threads. Good.

stream.py:123-143 — _score_trace now has explicit has_uploads judge instructions ('score grounded answers 9-10, refusals ≤2'). Effective in practice; judge_scores best_index now consistently points to local_retrieval in tests.

stream.py:343-366 — Defensive hard-override (filename or 6+-char chunk-token match → force best_index=local + score ≥9.5) is the right belt-and-suspenders fix. Heuristic uses lowercased substring containment over up to 200 chunk tokens; cheap and reliable. Consider: cap chunk_tokens to top-N by length/uniqueness for very large uploads.
stream.py:384-391 — Refiner system now explicitly forbids refusing/redacting uploaded content. Combined with the override, the final_answer faithfully includes the uploaded token.

Carryover from iter-4: SemanticCache.add() is still per-user (not per-thread). Current design is correct since cache.add is skipped on upload threads, but if you ever broaden caching, key by (user_id, thread_id) or invalidate on upload.
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_uploads_regression.py (new — 4 regression tests for iter-4 CRITICAL bugs)
seed_data_creation: Each regression test creates a fresh thread + small text upload under the admin account (filename has random suffix; content includes a per-run unique token like BANANA-PHONE-XXXXXX). 5-6 small test threads + uploads remain in the admin sidebar. Harmless; main agent may purge if desired.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Run `REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL=<public-url> pytest /app/backend/tests/test_uploads.py /app/backend/tests/test_uploads_regression.py -v` — both files together cover the upload pipeline (auth, text/PDF/JPEG upload, list, delete, cascade, 415, 413), the RAG happy path with the BANANA-PHONE token, AND the two iter-4 regressions (cache skipped on upload threads, judge picks local_retrieval, cross-thread isolation, no-uploads cache still works). The new file uses module-scoped fixtures and per-test unique tokens so it is rerunnable without manual DB cleanup.
rca of the issue: Iteration-4 had two compounding CRITICAL bugs. Fix verification: (1) Judge mis-scoring — fixed in chat/stream.py::_score_trace by adding a has_uploads code path that explicitly tells the judge to score grounded/citing answers 9-10 and refusals ≤2, PLUS a hard post-judge override that boosts local_retrieval to ≥9.5 whenever its answer references the uploaded filename or any 6+-char token from retrieved chunks. The refiner system prompt was also updated to forbid refusal/redaction when has_uploads. (2) Semantic-cache pollution — fixed by gating both cache.search() (line 204) and cache.add() (line 438-439) on has_uploads. Both fixes verified by black-box SSE-stream tests asserting on cache_check.hit, done.cache_hit, judge_scores.best_index, and final_answer content. No remaining critical issues.
